rulings.Case Title: KATHRYN ZUSCHIN VS. NEW CALIFORNIA LAND COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendnats' New California Land Company, Brown, Darin Richards' Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatory 17.1
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, March 26, 2012, Line 2. Defendants' New California Land Company and Brown, Darin Richards' Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatory 17.1. Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. = (302/PTJ)

rulings.Case Title: ELAINE IKE et al VS. FORD MOTOR COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Application To Seal Settlement Amount Listed In Th Memo Of P & A And Dec. Of H. Grant Law In Support Of Their Mtn For Order To Determine Good Faith Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, March 26, 2012, Line 3. DEFENDANTS FORD MOTOR COMPANY AND SOUTH CITY MOTORS, INC SUED HEREIN AS DBA S&C FORD'S Application To Seal Settlement Amount Listed In The Memo Of P & A And Declaration Of H. Grant Law In Support Of Their Motion For Order To Determine Good Faith Settlement. Denied. The interests supporting the sealing of the settlement amount do not overcome the interests which underlie the right of public access to court records. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: BANK OF GUAM, INC., A GUAM CORPORATION VS. EAST WEST BANK, INC., A FEDERALLY CHARTERED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, March 26, 2012, Line 14. CROSS DEFENDANT BANK OF GUAM, INC., A GUAM CORPORATION'S MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT. Denied as to the first and second causes of action and granted as to the third and fourth causes of action. The first cause of action is not based in any part on protected activity. The second cause of action is based in part on non-incidental protected activity, but Cross-Complainant has shown the required minimal to avoid dismissal. The third and fourth causes of action are based on protected activity and Cross-Complainant has not shown the require minimal merit to avoid dismissal. Refusing to consent to the sale of the loans was within Cross-Defendant's rights under the Loan Participation Agreement. Cross-Defendant is awarded attorney's fees pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure 425.16(c)(1). Although Cross-Defendant is only successful in striking two out of four causes of action, it is the prevailing party because the results of the motion were not so insignificant that defendant did not achieve any practical benefit from bringing the motion. However, Cross-Defendant is only entitled to fees in the amount of $7562.50, which is an amount commensurate with the extent that the motion changed the nature and character of the lawsuit in a practical way. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JERUSALEM CHURCH OF GOD IN CHRIST, A CALIFORNIA VS. TIMOTHY JONES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Regarding Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, March 26, 2012, Line 25. Order To Show Cause Regarding Preliminary Injunction. Hearing required per plaintiff's statement that it contests the following tentative ruling: Denied. Plaintiff has not shown by preponderance of the evidence that Pastor Galbraith had the authority to terminate the board members and to remove them as members of the Church. Defendants dispute that plaintiff has such authority. The Constitution of the national Church expressly provides that a local church may establish its own rules not inconsistent with the rules of the national Church. Nothing in the Constitution precludes a local church from establishing rules that bar a pastor from removing board members or terminating their membership in the local church. Moreover, there is a legitimate dispute whether the board had the authority to terminate Pastor Galbraith. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DYNAELECTRIC COMPANY, A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al VS. OLD REPUBLIC INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Moption To Compel Arb
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, March 26, 2012, Line 17. DEFENDANT OLD REPUBLIC INSURANCE COMPANY'S Notice Of Motion To Compel Arbitration. Granted. Plaintiff is required to arbitrate the dispute. The word "resolved" connotes finality, not something short of finality. Moreover, the parties' actions prior to the dispute arising are more suggestive that they believed the arbitration to be binding rather than "non-binding." The action is stayed pending the conclusion of the arbitration. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)
